title: Introduce _vendor namespace key in load_config/save_config
body:
## Summary

Refactor `load_config()` and `save_config()` across all framework commands so registry fields are nested under a `_vendor` key in `configs/<vendor>.json`.

## Scope

**Edit `templates/install`:**
- `save_config()` writes registry fields (`repo`, `install_branch`, `protected`, `allowed`, `private`, `automerge`, `dogfood`) under `_vendor` key
- `load_config()` reads `_vendor` key from each `configs/<vendor>.json`, returns the same `{"vendors": {"name": {...}}}` shape to callers (internal API unchanged)
- When writing a new vendor config, preserve any existing top-level keys (project config) — only replace `_vendor`

**Edit `templates/check`:**
- `load_config()` updated to read from `_vendor` key

**Edit `templates/remove`:**
- `save_config()` deletes the whole `configs/<vendor>.json` (no change needed — removing vendor removes the entire file)

## Backwards Compatibility

- If `configs/<vendor>.json` has no `_vendor` key (pre-migration), treat all top-level keys as registry fields (same as gv-a34c behavior)
- Migration from flat → `_vendor` handled in a separate task

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `save_config()` nests registry fields under `_vendor` key
- [ ] `load_config()` reads from `_vendor` key, returns same internal shape
- [ ] Writing `_vendor` does not clobber top-level project config keys
- [ ] Backwards compat: flat configs (no `_vendor` key) still load correctly
- [ ] Tests updated in test_install.py, test_check.py, test_remove.py

title: Merge per-vendor project configs into configs/<vendor>.json
body:
## Summary

Add a migration step that detects existing per-vendor project config files (e.g. `.pearls/config.json`, `.semver/config.json`) and merges their contents into the top level of `.vendored/configs/<vendor>.json`, alongside the `_vendor` registry key.

## Scope

**Edit `templates/install`:**
- After gv-a34c's monolithic config migration runs, check each vendor for a legacy project config file
- Detection heuristic: look for `config.json` inside the vendor's old install directory (derived from `_vendor.protected` patterns or known convention `.<vendor-name>/config.json`)
- Merge: read legacy config, write all keys to the top level of `configs/<vendor>.json` (never overwrite `_vendor`)
- Delete the old config file after successful merge
- Log migration to stderr

**Known legacy configs:**
- `.pearls/config.json` → top-level keys in `.vendored/configs/pearls.json`
- `.semver/config.json` → top-level keys in `.vendored/configs/semver.json`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Migration detects legacy project configs in old vendor directories
- [ ] Contents merged into top level of `configs/<vendor>.json`
- [ ] `_vendor` key is not affected by the merge
- [ ] Old config files removed after successful migration
- [ ] Idempotent: running twice doesn't duplicate or corrupt
- [ ] Tests: merge correctness, no clobber of `_vendor`, cleanup, idempotency

title: Update vendor tools to read project config from .vendored/configs/
body:
## Summary

Update vendored tool implementations so they read their project config from `.vendored/configs/<vendor>.json` (top-level keys, ignoring `_vendor`) instead of their old dot-directory config files.

## Scope

**pearls (`prl.py`):**
- `load_config()` currently reads `.pearls/config.json`
- Change to read `.vendored/configs/pearls.json`, parse top-level keys (ignore `_vendor`)
- Fallback: if `.vendored/configs/pearls.json` doesn't exist, try `.pearls/config.json` (backwards compat)
- `load_prefix()` similarly updated

**git-semver (`git-semver`):**
- `load_config()` currently reads `.semver/config.json`
- Change to read `.vendored/configs/semver.json`, parse top-level keys (ignore `_vendor`)
- Fallback: if `.vendored/configs/semver.json` doesn't exist, try `.semver/config.json`

## Note

These are upstream vendor repo changes. In this (dogfood) repo, the vendored copies will be updated directly. Upstream repos should be updated separately to maintain the new contract.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] prl.py reads project config from `.vendored/configs/pearls.json`
- [ ] git-semver reads project config from `.vendored/configs/semver.json`
- [ ] Both fall back to old location if new config doesn't exist
- [ ] Both ignore `_vendor` key when loading project config
- [ ] Existing tests updated or new tests added for config loading

title: Update vendor compliance guide for _vendor key and project config
body:
## Summary

Update the vendor compliance guide (created in gv-a34c.3) to document the `_vendor` key convention and how vendors should read project config from the unified config file.

## Scope

**Edit `docs/vendor-install-dir-guide.md`:**
- Document `_vendor` key: what it contains, who owns it, why underscore prefix
- Document project config: top-level keys in `configs/<vendor>.json` are vendor-owned
- Show config loading example: read file, ignore `_vendor`, use remaining keys
- Document fallback behavior for backwards compat with old dot-dir configs
- Update the per-vendor config schema example to show `_vendor` + project keys

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Guide documents `_vendor` key purpose and ownership
- [ ] Guide shows how vendors read project config from unified file
- [ ] Guide includes code example for config loading with `_vendor` filtering
- [ ] Guide documents fallback to legacy dot-dir config locations

title: Clean up empty vendor dot-directories after config migration
body:
## Summary

After project configs have migrated into `.vendored/configs/` and vendor code into `.vendored/pkg/`, the old dot-directories (`.pearls/`, `.semver/`) may contain only data files (e.g. `issues.jsonl`) or may be empty. Clean up directories that are no longer needed.

## Scope

- After migration, check if old vendor dot-directories contain only files that have been moved or are now tracked elsewhere
- `.pearls/`: `prl.py`, `merge-driver.py`, `AGENTS.md` move to `pkg/`; `config.json` merges into `configs/`; `issues.jsonl` and `archive/` are data — these may stay or move
- `.semver/`: `git-semver` moves to `pkg/`; `config.json` merges into `configs/`; `.version` is data
- For data files (`issues.jsonl`, `.version`), decide: move to `pkg/<vendor>/` or keep in dot-dir
- If dot-dir is empty after moves, remove it
- Update `.gitignore`, `.gitattributes` references as needed

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Old dot-directories cleaned up or documented as intentionally retained
- [ ] No orphaned config files remain in old locations
- [ ] Data file locations decided and documented
- [ ] .gitignore/.gitattributes updated if paths changed

title: Data file locations undecided and undocumented after dot-dir cleanup
